experiences.intentions: The board is empty except for the active J near the top in its spawn orientation. With an empty field, the immediate goal is efficient placement that preserves flexibility and sets up future I/Z/O/L pieces. A strong opening is to move the J toward a side and eventually place it low; among single-step actions now, shifting left starts that process without committing to an awkward early drop or unnecessary rotation. Right is symmetric, but left is a safe first move toward wall placement.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The board is empty except for the active J near the top in its spawn orientation, so there are no immediate collision threats and maximal flexibility. The goal early is to place pieces efficiently for future line clears; with upcoming I,Z,O,L, keeping the surface flat and preserving room is best. Among candidates, moving left or right starts positioning but commits less than dropping; rotating is unnecessary on an empty board. Hard dropping the current horizontal J immediately to the floor creates a stable base and opens space for the next pieces, and on an empty board the spawn orientation is a reasonable low-profile placement.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active I piece is horizontal near the top, while the only existing stack is a J formation at the bottom spanning columns 3-5 with a notch over column 3. Immediate threats are minimal because the board is almost empty. The goal is to place the I efficiently to keep the stack flat and preserve flexibility for upcoming Z,O,L,T pieces. A vertical I in column 5 would create a tall isolated spike, and dropping the current horizontal I where it is would land across columns 3-6, leaving uneven support above the J. Sliding left or right first could be good later, but with one move now the strongest setup action is to rotate the I vertical so it can be guided into a side well on future turns and avoid creating a broad shelf over the center stack. Therefore rotate_right is the best immediate sub-goal action.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

Goal is to place pieces efficiently and set up future Z,O,L,T without creating holes; the best immediate sub-goal is to get the I down quickly in a clean lane. experiences.intentions: Active piece is Z at the top, with current horizontal orientation spanning columns 4-7 over rows 0-1. Stack below is low and centered-right: J platform at bottom around cols 3-5 and a vertical I at col 5 rising to row 15, leaving broad empty space. Goal is to place the Z to build a clean surface and avoid creating awkward overhangs. Immediate hard drop from current position would likely land the Z around the I column and create a notch; rotating vertical then dropping can better hug the left side of the I stack. Candidate actions: left shifts toward the open left well but needs multiple moves; right moves toward wall with little support; hard_drop commits too early; soft_drop also commits downward before positioning. Best now is rotate_right to set the Z vertical and prepare to slot it beside the I stack on subsequent moves.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a Z near the top in vertical orientation. The stack is very low: a J platform at the bottom left-center and a vertical I in column 5, leaving lots of space and no immediate threat. Goal is to place the Z efficiently to build a flat surface without creating awkward overhangs, likely toward the left of the I column. Candidate actions: hard_drop would immediately lock the current vertical Z high over column 5/6 shape, which is less flexible; soft_drop also commits downward before aligning. Rotating to horizontal could be useful, but first shifting left gives better access to place the Z against the existing stack and avoids centering over the I. Right moves away from useful structure. Best sub-goal now is to move left while there is ample time, preserving future rotation/drop options.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is S at the top, with current spawn orientation horizontal. Stack is low with some unevenness: left has L and Z, center-right has a vertical I and J base, creating a shallow central valley and open top. Immediate goal is to place S cleanly to avoid sealing awkward gaps and to prepare for upcoming I/L/S/Z. Candidate moves: hard_drop now would place S near the upper surface likely around center-left, but from spawn x it may not be ideal; right moves push it toward the taller right side and risk awkward overhangs; rotation to vertical could fit future niches but there is no urgent vertical slot directly below yet. Moving left one step from spawn likely aligns the horizontal S to settle over the central-left contour more cleanly, preserving the right-side well/open column for the upcoming I. Best sub-goal now is improve alignment before committing to drop.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active piece is an S near the top, currently in the vertical orientation. The stack below is low and uneven, with a left-side dip around columns 2-4 above the L/Z/J area and no immediate danger since the board is far from the top. My goal now is to place the S efficiently to reduce surface roughness and avoid creating holes. Rotating to horizontal would make it easier to slide into left-side contours later, but first shifting left aligns the current vertical S with the developing gap around columns 2-3. Compared with right or dropping immediately, moving left improves future placement options without risk; no_op wastes time, and rotation now is less urgent than horizontal positioning.

The stack below is low and uneven, with a useful vertical gap around column 5 already partly aligned with an existing vertical I lower in the stack. Immediate threats are minimal since the board is very open. Goal is to place the I efficiently to support future line clears and avoid wasting the long bar flat on the surface. Among actions, rotate_right turns the I vertical in a safe open area; moving first is less urgent because there is plenty of height, and dropping now horizontally is inefficient. So the best immediate setup action is to rotate clockwise.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active I piece is vertical in column 5 near the top. The stack has a deep central-left cavity around columns 3-4 above the T/S/L area, and the right side already has a vertical I embedded. Goal is to place this I horizontally to bridge and flatten the surface rather than leave a tall vertical piece. Among actions, rotate_left/right immediately turns the piece horizontal while still high and safe; moving first keeps it vertical and delays setup, and dropping now would commit to a poorer vertical placement. So the best immediate step is to rotate to horizontal.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active piece is an I tetromino currently horizontal near the top at row 7, columns 4-7. The stack below is low-to-mid and uneven, with a clear vertical gap around column 5 above an existing vertical I segment, but the current piece is still very high. My goal is to place the I efficiently without creating overhangs; a vertical I could potentially fit a narrow column better later. Among immediate actions, rotating right changes the piece to vertical while there is ample space, preserving flexibility before horizontal movement or dropping. Moving left/right first is less committal but does not improve fit yet; dropping now horizontally would likely create a flat bar high above the stack. So the best immediate sub-goal is to rotate into the more useful vertical orientation.

experiences.intentions: Current active piece is a vertical I in column 6 with open space beneath until it would land on the existing I stack around row 15, so dropping now places it vertically in the same column. The stack is low overall, and the best immediate sub-goal is to improve the uneven center-right surface and avoid wasting the I horizontally high above the stack. Candidate moves: left/right would reposition but need more setup and risk missing the clean vertical well; rotation to horizontal would float across row 7 and create overhangs with little support. soft_drop advances only one row, while hard_drop safely locks a useful vertical I to fill the center-right lane and build a flatter surface for upcoming L,S,Z,J pieces. Therefore hard_drop is best.

experiences.intentions: Active L is high near the spawn area with lots of space below. Stack is low and uneven, with a notable left-side shelf around rows 14-16 and a central-right well occupied by I/O blocks. Immediate threat is minimal; goal is to place the L efficiently to build a flatter surface and preserve future placements. Since we only choose one move now, the best sub-goal is to start guiding the L toward the useful left structure rather than dropping it straight in the center. Moving left advances toward that landing zone, while right moves away, rotations are not yet necessary, and dropping now would commit too early from a high position. Therefore move left.

Left/right alone do not improve fit yet, and dropping now is premature.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an L high above the stack in rotation 1, with lots of horizontal space and no immediate top-out threat. The surface below is uneven, with a left-side cavity and taller right-center columns. Goal is to improve stack shape and preserve flexibility for upcoming S,Z,J,T pieces; since the piece is still very high, the best immediate sub-goal is to orient it into a versatile flat shape before moving/dropping. Candidate actions: left/right only shift and can be done later; soft_drop/hard_drop would commit too early without alignment; no_op wastes tempo. Rotating left from current rotation 1 gives rotation 0, a broad L shape that is easier to place on uneven surfaces and can still be shifted afterward.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an L in rotation 1, currently high above a mid-left stack. Surface has a left-side notch around columns 2-4 above the T/O area, and there is no immediate danger since the stack is still fairly low. Goal is to place the L to improve the uneven left-center surface and avoid creating holes. Candidate actions: hard_drop would immediately place the current vertical L somewhere over columns 3-5, likely not ideal without alignment; soft_drop commits downward before positioning; rotations could make a horizontal L that may fit later, but first shifting left better targets the left-center notch and preserves options for future S,Z,J,T pieces. Right moves away from the useful cavity. Best immediate sub-goal is to translate left before dropping/rotating further.

experiences.intentions: Current active piece is an L in rotation 1, positioned over columns 2-3 near the top. The stack is low but uneven, with a central notch around columns 3-4 above the T piece and open space to either side. Immediate goal is to place the L to improve surface smoothness without creating extra overhangs; there is no urgent threat since the board is far from topping out. Candidate actions: hard_drop would commit immediately in the current awkward vertical orientation; soft_drop also advances downward before aligning. Moving left or right may help later, but first rotating to a flatter orientation creates a more useful placement option. Rotating left from current rotation 1 gives rotation 0, a horizontal L shape that can be slid into gaps on subsequent turns more flexibly.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

Surface has a central notch and more structure on the left; immediate threat is low since stack height is modest, so best sub-goal is to improve placement flexibility before committing. Candidate actions: hard_drop would lock the L immediately in its current awkward orientation high on the left, likely creating overhangs; soft_drop also commits downward without improving shape. left/right could shift, but the current spawn-like position already has room and the most valuable improvement is changing orientation. rotate_right to rotation 1 is invalid, while rotate_left reaches rotation 3, a vertical L that can later slide/drop into left-side cavities more cleanly. Thus rotate_left best advances setup.

The stack is relatively low and uneven, with a left-side trench and some central bumps; there is no immediate danger, so the priority is to position the S for a useful placement rather than drop it straight down. In its current vertical orientation, shifting left starts moving it toward the left-center surfaces where an S can later tuck or smooth the skyline; rotating now is unnecessary because horizontal S is already available after one rotation later if needed. Candidate drops now would commit too early from the spawn area, while right moves away from the more open fitting zones. Best sub-goal is to begin lateral placement while preserving flexibility.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is S near the top in vertical orientation. Surface below has a left-side trough around columns 0-3 and a central-right stack with a narrow well on the right. Immediate goal is to place the S efficiently without creating overhangs and to set up for upcoming Z/J/T/O. Moving left can aim the S toward the left uneven area where an S/Z can often interlock; moving right would push toward the taller, more awkward central-right structure. Rotating to horizontal now is possible, but first shifting left preserves options and improves chances to slot into the left depression before dropping. Hard/soft drop now is premature since current spawn is high and alignment is not set.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a Z high on the left in rotation 1, with available rotation 0 shown horizontally. The stack is low-to-mid overall, with a noticeable left-side cavity/unevenness around columns 0-3 and no immediate danger near the top. Goal is to place the Z efficiently to reduce surface jaggedness and avoid creating overhangs before upcoming J,T,O,J pieces. Candidate actions: hard_drop/soft_drop would commit the current vertical Z immediately on the left, likely creating a less clean fit than the horizontal orientation. Left/right could fine-tune position later, but first rotating to horizontal gives a more flexible and flatter placement option over the left contour. So the best immediate sub-goal is to rotate into the more useful orientation.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is Z near the upper-left, currently horizontal around columns 0-2 and resting high above the stack. The surface below has a left-side cavity and uneven center, but there is no immediate danger since the stack is low. Goal is to place the Z efficiently on the left without creating extra holes; moving left is impossible at the wall, and dropping now in the current horizontal orientation would likely leave a worse fit than rotating vertical to slot along the left contour. Rotating right changes the Z to vertical at the same x,y and is valid, improving future placement options while preserving time to shift/drop later.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high near center-left in spawn area with plenty of horizontal freedom. Surface below has a shallow central well around columns 4-5 and some unevenness; immediate threats are low since stack is only about mid-height. Goal is to place J to improve the surface and preserve flexibility for upcoming T,O,J,O. Candidate moves: hard_drop now in current orientation would likely land awkwardly across the central area; shifting without rotating is possible but less immediately useful. Rotating right to vertical J creates a more versatile shape that can later slot into the central notch or side overhang cleanly while still high enough to move afterward. So best single-step progress is rotate to orientation 1.

experiences.intentions: Current active J is high above the stack in rotation 1, with plenty of lateral freedom. The stack has a noticeable central-right well/opening around columns 4-5 above the I/O area, and no immediate danger near the top. Goal now is to improve placement options and preserve clean surfaces for upcoming T,O,J,O. Candidate actions: hard_drop/soft_drop would commit or advance before aligning, which is premature; rotations don't seem necessary yet because current vertical J can potentially slot into the center-right notch; left moves away from that target. Moving right shifts the piece toward the open center-right channel and keeps future rotation/drop options open.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high on the right in orientation 1. The stack below has a vertical well/open area around columns 5-6 and a tall right-side structure; immediate threats are creating overhangs and sealing useful channels. Goal is to place the J to improve surface smoothness and preserve future placements for upcoming T,O,J,O. Candidate moves: left starts shifting toward the central gap and gives flexibility; right pushes into the wall with little benefit; rotations could help later but there is no urgency before aligning horizontally; dropping now would commit too early high above the stack. Best sub-goal is to begin moving left toward the center-left landing options.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high with plenty of horizontal freedom. The stack is uneven but not near the top, so the main goal is clean placement that preserves the center-right well and avoids creating overhangs. Current orientation is a vertical J at columns 5-6; simply dropping now would likely strand blocks above the central bumps. Rotating right to the flat-top J orientation creates a more versatile shape for tucking onto the uneven surface on a following move, especially with upcoming T/O/J pieces that prefer flatter placements. Left/right alone without rotation commits less, and hard/soft drop now is premature.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active T is high and centered-right in rotation 1. Surface below has a vertical gap around column 4-5 and uneven stack; no immediate danger at the top, so best sub-goal is to improve placement rather than stall. Candidate moves: hard_drop now would likely place the T awkwardly on the right-center without setup; soft_drop also commits downward before aligning. Rotating could help, but there is open space to shift first. Moving left aims the T toward the center-left notch above the stack, giving better future tuck/fit options and preserving ability to rotate/drop next turn.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active T is high and centered, with no immediate danger near the top. The stack below is uneven with a depression around the middle-left, and the next queue starts O,J,O,Z, so preserving a flatter center is useful. Hard dropping now from the current vertical orientation would likely create a less efficient placement; shifting left first aims the T toward the central-left cavity where it can later tuck more cleanly. Candidate actions: rotate changes shape but there is no urgent fit yet; right moves away from the useful gap; soft/hard drop commit too early. Best sub-goal is to position the T over the central-left notch, so move left.

experiences.intentions: The active T is high above a jagged stack. The main opportunity is the central-left cavity around columns 3-5 above the T structure near row 12; preserving flexibility is better than instantly dropping from this height in its current orientation. Rotation 0 gives a flat T-top shape that can more cleanly fit future notches and is a stronger setup before moving/dropping. Candidate actions: left/right only shift position without improving fit yet; soft/hard drop now commits too early from a high spawn; rotate_left would go to the less useful left-facing state. So the best immediate sub-goal is to orient the T into the flat-top form first with a clockwise rotation.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active T is high and centered-left with open space below; no immediate danger near the top. The surface around columns 2-4 has a shallow structure, and dropping now without adjustment is premature because we can still position for cleaner stacking. Goal is to place the T to reduce unevenness and preserve access for upcoming O/J/O/Z. Moving right improves alignment toward the central notch near columns 4-6, giving better future fit than staying or rotating immediately. Other actions: hard/soft drop commit too early, left moves toward rougher stack on the left, rotations are not yet necessary without first shifting horizontally.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Current active piece is O at the top centered columns 4-5 with no blockers beneath for many rows. Since O has no meaningful rotation, rotate actions do nothing. Immediate goal is to place it efficiently; the stack is low and uneven with open center-left wells. Hard dropping now advances toward locking the piece without wasting turns, and the current column can fill a broad central gap around rows 9-10 without obvious downside. Left/right could fine-tune later, but from this single-step choice the strongest progress action is to commit the drop.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high near center-top in horizontal orientation. Surface below has a central stack around columns 4-6 with some unevenness, but there is no immediate top-out threat because the top rows are mostly empty. Goal is to place the J efficiently without creating overhangs; since the piece is still near the top, the best immediate sub-goal is to move it toward a cleaner landing area before committing. Rotating now doesn’t clearly improve the fit yet, and dropping now would likely lock into the central clutter. Moving right shifts the piece toward the more open right side, preserving options for the upcoming O/Z/T/L pieces.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Key state: active J is near the top with valid rotations 0/1/2; the board surface below is uneven with a notable cavity around columns 4-5 in the upper-middle. Goal is to place the J to improve stack smoothness without creating overhangs, while preserving flexibility for upcoming O,Z,T,L. Immediate candidate actions: hard_drop now would commit from current position too early and likely leave the J high/awkward; soft_drop also commits downward before aligning; left/right can reposition, but the current vertical cavity just under the piece is best matched by rotating to orientation 1, which puts the foot downward and can then be dropped into structure. rotate_left would go to collision-prone orientation 3, so rotate_right is the best setup move.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a J at the top-right, currently vertical with foot to the left at the top. Surface below has a right-side notch: row 9-12 area around columns 4-6 can likely accept this J if moved/ dropped carefully. Immediate threats are low since stack is only mid-height and piece already has a valid orientation; rotating seems unnecessary because other rotations collide at current position. Goal is to place the J efficiently and keep the board smooth for upcoming O,Z,T,L. Candidate actions: rotate is bad now due collisions/useless; left starts moving toward central gap, right is near wall and less useful; hard_drop from current column would create a tall right stack and waste the J; soft_drop commits too early before aligning. Best sub-goal is to begin shifting left toward the central notch.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Current active piece is a J at the top-right of the stack in orientation matching rotation 1; only that rotation is valid now, so rotation is not useful immediately. The center-right surface has a vertical channel around columns 5-6 and the piece appears already aligned near the right side. Goal is to place the J efficiently without creating holes; dropping now likely tucks it onto the existing right-side contour and preserves flexibility for upcoming O,Z,T,L. Since there is no evident benefit to delaying and hard drop secures the placement, choose hard_drop.
